input.prompt: Analyze the last 100 lines of the system logs for critical errors

output: STABILITY_MODE: Analyze the last 100 lines of the system logs for ...

task.title: Retrieve Last 100 System Log Lines
task.description: Access system log buffer and extract the most recent 100 entries for analysis.

solution.approach: Identify the system log source (e.g., /var/log/syslog, journalctl, or custom log file). Determine whether logs are managed by a service like rsyslog or systemd-journald. Understand the format and structure of log entries to ensure correct parsing when retrieving the last 100 lines.
solution.complexity: O(1) for direct file access; O(n) for querying journald with filtering

solution.approach: Design a command-line interface approach using tail command for file-based logs or journalctl with --lines=100 for systemd logs. Ensure output is captured in a structured format (e.g., plain text or JSON if supported) for downstream analysis.
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is 100, constant time for fixed limit

solution.approach: Execute the appropriate command based on log source. If using systemd: run 'journalctl -n 100'. Otherwise, execute 'tail -n 100 /var/log/syslog' or equivalent. Capture stdout into a variable or temporary file for further processing.
solution.algorithm: Command execution with redirection or piping

solution.approach: Verify that exactly 100 lines were retrieved. Check timestamp order (most recent last or first depending on system). Validate log integrity (no truncation, malformed entries). Confirm output matches expected format and contains meaningful log data.
solution.algorithm: wc -l <output>; grep pattern checks; timestamp validation

solution.approach: Identify the system log source (e.g., /var/log/syslog, journalctl, or custom log file). Determine whether logs are managed by a service like rsyslog or systemd-journald. Understand the format and structure of log entries to ensure correct parsing when retrieving the last 100 lines.
solution.complexity: O(1) for direct file access; O(n) for querying journald with filtering
